name: Beryllium Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Beryllium. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power range for Beryllium oxide layer removal

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength optimized for Beryllium thermal absorption characteristics

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning control

repetitionRate.description: High repetition rate for efficient cleaning with minimal thermal damage

fluenceThreshold.description: Fluence threshold for Beryllium oxide removal without substrate damage

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of surface contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Number of cleaning passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Beam overlap percentage for uniform cleaning pattern

power.description: Typical range for 1064 nm Nd:YAG lasers in metal cleaning to balance efficiency and heat input; starting at 150 W for Beryllium due to high reflectivity (~70% at 1064 nm) reducing absorption; max avoids substrate melting (1287°C) per studies on reflective metals (e.g., SPIE Proc. Vol. 10912, 2019). Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs.

scan_speed.description: Range for effective contaminant removal without excessive heat accumulation; 1000 mm/s recommended for optimal balance on Beryllium's high thermal conductivity (216 W/mK); slower (200-500 mm/s) for heavy contamination, faster (up to 3000 mm/s) for light. Source: Laserax equipment manuals and J. Laser Appl. 32, 022064 (2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime (5-100 ns) typical for industrial cleaning to ablate contaminants while minimizing heat-affected zone (HAZ); 20 ns recommended for Beryllium to limit thermal diffusion given its conductivity; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-precise but costlier applications. Source: Laser physics review in Opt. Lasers Eng. 120, 102-115 (2019).

repetition_rate.description: 10-100 kHz for cleaning applications; 50 kHz recommended for Beryllium to achieve fast processing without significant heat buildup (high freq increases speed but risks accumulation on reflective surfaces); trade-off noted in ablation efficiency. Source: Academic study in Appl. Surf. Sci. 475, 245-252 (2019).

energy_density.description: Fluence range for contaminant removal on metals; Beryllium ablation threshold ~2-3 J/cm² at 1064 nm, so 1.5 J/cm² recommended for safe cleaning below damage; higher for heavy contamination but conservative due to reflectivity. Source: Ablation threshold studies in J. Phys. D: Appl. Phys. 52, 395301 (2019).

spot_size.description: 100-500 μm beam diameter for cleaning; 200 μm recommended for Beryllium in industrial surface prep to ensure uniform energy delivery without pinpoint damage; smaller for fine details, larger for broad areas. Source: Coherent Inc. laser cleaning system specifications.

pass_count.description: 1-5 passes typical for complete removal; 2 passes recommended for Beryllium to achieve thorough cleaning of contaminants while avoiding cumulative thermal stress; single pass for light layers, multi for heavy. Source: Industry case studies in Proc. SPIE 11080, 110800M (2019).

overlap_ratio.description: 30-80% overlap for uniform coverage in scanning; 50% recommended for Beryllium to ensure even fluence distribution and prevent gaps, optimizing for its reflective properties. Source: Process optimization in Surf. Coat. Technol. 378, 124-130 (2019).
